programmes.show_description: Adam tours the meatiest meals the US has to offer! He heads to North Carolina for some quintessential barbecue and Idaho for mammoth burgers. S1, Ep2
Durham. Adam Richman returns to Durham, North Carolina, for burgers and barbeque, and to recruit a team for the Doughman Challenge: a four-member, food-and-sport relay race. S2, Ep3
Bringin' It Home. Guy visits a family restaurant cooking Italian dishes in California. Then, in North Carolina, he goes to a neighbourhood favourite that serves delicious fish tacos. S9, Ep4
Homegrown And Homemade. In South Carolina, Guy Fieri visits a farmer's market. Plus, in Lackawanna, New York, he tries a lasagne loaded with stuffed peppers at an Italian restaurant. S9, Ep2
Creepy Crawly Cake And Haunted House. It's Halloween and Buddy's eldest son wants a haunted house to match his spooky cake. Carlo's crew create an intricate carved pumpkin cake. S9, Ep11
Dreams Come True. Rapper Fat Joe wants a cake to celebrate his new hit single, and Buddy's design is as good as gold! Plus, the team create a Gingerbread Man-inspired cake. S9, Ep12
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Jazz. The bikers return to New Orleans, where Simon tries to convince Dave that jazz can be enjoyable. Plus, they cook classic local dishes, including jambalaya and gumbo. S1, Ep5
Lancashire. The Bikers start their northern road trip in Lancashire. They get breakfast at a food truck in the Trough of Bowland before heading to a gastropub famous for their pies. S1, Ep1
Quick And Easy: New Favorites. Ree Drummond decides it's time for out with the old and in with the new. She makes a flank steak and mouth-watering three-cheese waffle hash browns. S23, Ep5
Quick And Easy: Take Five. Ree Drummond makes four meals with just five ingredients. For breakfast she makes a Mexican classic of chilaquiles and a fabulously fast steak for lunch. S23, Ep6
Sicily. Jamie travels to the island of Sicily in search of sensational home-cooked recipes. Plus, he is inspired to cook an epic tuna, prawn and pistachio spaghetti. S1, Ep5
Tuscany. Jamie is in Tuscany to track down the grandmothers who hold the key to the region's farmhouse cooking. And, he makes a beef stew with homemade pappardelle pasta. S1, Ep6
Training Day Dinner. Ina helps serve up a meal for local volunteers. She prepares grilled bread with prosciutto, potato salad, barbecue pork ribs and pear and cranberry crostata. S7, Ep1
Barefoot Bistro. Ina is demystifying French classics including mussel bisque, apricot butter and apple tarts. Plus, she offers useful tips for cooking French cuisine. S7, Ep2
Pastry. With only six bakers left in the tent, the stakes increase as they face the pastry round. The remaining bakers bring the old fashioned suet pudding bang up to date. S4, Ep7
Quarter Final. It is the quarter final and now there are just five bakers left, and they face the challenges of unconventional flours and unusual desserts. S4, Ep8
James travels to Bresse, a region in France he's never visited before. He visits a chicken farm and museum and meets one of his culinary heroes, George Blanc. S1, Ep9
James travels to a region known for its Pinot Noir, Burgundy. He retraces Keith Floyd's visits 30 years ago and stops at the same vineyard for a personal tour. S1, Ep10
Lancashire. The Bikers start their northern road trip in Lancashire. They get breakfast at a food truck in the Trough of Bowland before heading to a gastropub famous for their pies. S1, Ep1
Quick And Easy: New Favorites. Ree Drummond decides it's time for out with the old and in with the new. She makes a flank steak and mouth-watering three-cheese waffle hash browns. S23, Ep5
Quick And Easy: Take Five. Ree Drummond makes four meals with just five ingredients. For breakfast she makes a Mexican classic of chilaquiles and a fabulously fast steak for lunch. S23, Ep6
Sicily. Jamie travels to the island of Sicily in search of sensational home-cooked recipes. Plus, he is inspired to cook an epic tuna, prawn and pistachio spaghetti. S1, Ep5
Tuscany. Jamie is in Tuscany to track down the grandmothers who hold the key to the region's farmhouse cooking. And, he makes a beef stew with homemade pappardelle pasta. S1, Ep6
Training Day Dinner. Ina helps serve up a meal for local volunteers. She prepares grilled bread with prosciutto, potato salad, barbecue pork ribs and pear and cranberry crostata. S7, Ep1
Barefoot Bistro. Ina is demystifying French classics including mussel bisque, apricot butter and apple tarts. Plus, she offers useful tips for cooking French cuisine. S7, Ep2
Pastry. With only six bakers left in the tent, the stakes increase as they face the pastry round. The remaining bakers bring the old fashioned suet pudding bang up to date. S4, Ep7
Quarter Final. It is the quarter final and now there are just five bakers left, and they face the challenges of unconventional flours and unusual desserts. S4, Ep8
Newark, New Jersey. Casey Webb heads to his home state, New Jersey! He samples an iconic Italian hot dog, a Portuguese pork sandwich and takes part in a taco challenge. S5, Ep5
Brooklyn, New York. Casey Webb's quest takes him to Brooklyn for a barbeque and deli feast. Plus, he samples an Italian seafood staple and takes on a spicy shrimp challenge. S5, Ep6
Honest Mistake Or Foul Play? Adam Sobel seeks to publicly humiliate Matt Jennings, and Marjorie Meek-Bradley calls out elite competitor Mei Lin. S1, Ep6
Cajun. Si and Dave visit the hotbed of Cajun culture in Lafayette, Louisiana. There, the bikers sample crawfish and groove at a jam session. S1, Ep4
Jazz. The bikers return to New Orleans, where Simon tries to convince Dave that jazz can be enjoyable. Plus, they cook classic local dishes, including jambalaya and gumbo. S1, Ep5
Country. The duo head to Nashville, Tennessee, to learn about the roots of country music. Plus, they cook biscuits and gravy and visit the Grand Ole Opry stage. S1, Ep6
Pep Rally, Birthday Treat And Fancy Feet. Buddy gets into the school spirit with a cake for Sofia's school pep rally. Grace takes a special consultation for a 40th birthday cake. S9, Ep13
Saudi Arabia and Lobster Tail Feast. Carlo's team takes a cake order for delivery to all the way to Saudi Arabia. Back home a fight is brewing over the bakery's signature pastry. S9, Ep14

East Coast, West Coast. Guy hits both coasts for tasty dishes. In California, he grabs spicy pork and plantains and visits a bakery-restaurant serving up meatloaf in North Carolina. S28, Ep12
Beef And Bird. Guy Fieri loads up the meat! In New Hampshire, a funky joint puts Thai in their tacos, and a local gem in North Carolina fires up monster barbecue. S36, Ep5
Beef, Lamb And Pig. Guy Fieri hunts down meaty favourites in Kentucky and North Carolina. He enjoys an Irish pub's cottage pie, Asian-barbecue fusion and pimento-topped burgers. S26, Ep5
From Noodle To Strudel. Guy Fieri hits up some major deliciousness with sweet strudel in Boulder, Colorado, next-level Nepalese in Alberta and killer noodles in Memphis, Tennessee. S38, Ep11
Cooking With Friends. It's competition time as Ina and her friends, Eli Zabar and David Ziff, cook a Mediterranean-inspired meal. Plus, tasty three-berries pancakes for breakfast. S6, Ep3
Rehearsal Dinner. Ina prepares a fool proof menu for any special occasion. The buffet table includes tarragon potato salad, chunky blue cheese and vegetable coleslaw. S6, Ep4
Dare To Be Different. Ina adapts classic dishes by adding new flavours and altering the cooking method. She makes an easy Parmesan risotto and deconstructed strawberry shortcakes. S6, Ep5
Modern Comfort. Ina makes classic comfort foods. Her friend, Mary Giuliani, helps Ina to make miniature grilled cheese sandwich canapés, French toast pudding and a seafood pot pie. S6, Ep6
Jeffrey's Birthday Pop-Up. Ina throws Jeffrey a surprise birthday party. On the menu for the celebration is a Greek platter, spinach pie and a delicious raspberry and orange trifle. S6, Ep7
Brunch Bunch. Ina enlists the help of a local florist and photographer to create the perfect brunch setup. In the kitchen, she makes baked blintzes with fresh blueberry sauce. S6, Ep8
With the rising cost of food and energy, Jamie Oliver is on a mission to make delicious dishes that won't break the budget. S1, Ep1
The Ultimate Burger. Jamie Oliver takes the UK's favourite cuisine to the next level. The chef shows how to make his ultimate burger with his friend, DJ BBQ. S1, Ep1
Jamie Oliver masters Bolognese ravioli. Plus, he recreates the UK's favourite dish, chicken tikka masala - with a secret ingredient. S1, Ep2
Isles of Scilly. James starts his British foodie journey in the Scilly Isles, home of world-class seafood. He's joined by Michelin-starred chef, Galton Blackiston. S1, Ep1
Cornwall. James returns to one of his favourite places in the UK, Cornwall, with Michelin-starred chef, Galton Blackiston. Dishes include tasty seafood tapas. S1, Ep2
The Bikers transform everyday ingredients into comforting dishes. Bananas are made into a sophisticated tarte tatin and ricotta cheese is the basis for gnudi. S1, Ep9
Dave and Si cook dishes to feed a multitude with crowd-pleasing dishes. On the menu is salmon pie with spinach and hollandaise and chocolate and cherry buns. S1, Ep10
The Bikers cook the perfect meals for when it's cold outside. Warming dishes include oxtail soup, a vegetarian chestnut and mushroom pie, and treacle sponge. S1, Ep11
Dave and Si prepare dishes which evoke traditional values. They put a twist on a classic pudding, create a perfect teatime treat and combine two breakfast favourites. S1, Ep12
Street Eats. Adam goes to Tampa, NYC and L.A. to sample the country's best street carts. He tries tacos and waffles before taking on a six-pound burrito with 'Big Dawg' in L.A. S1, Ep18
St. Paul. Adam heads to St Paul, Minnesota where he tastes an authentic pizza pie. Later, he guides Jon through the Lucy Challenge at Tin Cup's, but will he succeed? S1, Ep19
Yorkshire Pudding. Adam ticks 'Yorkshire Pudding in Yorkshire' off his foodie bucket list. Then, he tries Yorkshire parkin, local rabbit, and a Yorkshire tea-inspired dessert. S1, Ep1
Sandwich. Adam heads to Kent to try a very special sandwich in Sandwich. There, he also tries Whitstable oysters, traditional fish and chips, and a new baked sensation, the cruffin. S1, Ep2
Sandwiches, Seafood And Sweets. Guy Fieri's getting into a killer Cuban sandwich in Baton Rouge, artisan croissants in Gainesville, and outrageous oxtail nachos in Tallahassee. S41, Ep7
Specialty Spots. Guy Fieri's grabbing finger-licking chicken in Gainesville, meatless gorditas in Dallas, and mouthwatering pies in Baton Rouge. S41, Ep8
Eastern Southern. Guy Fieri heads east to get a taste of the South. He checks out meatloaf in New Hampshire, extra-spicy shrimp in North Carolina and seafood stew in Florida. S36, Ep3
Beef And Bird. Guy Fieri loads up the meat! In New Hampshire, a funky joint puts Thai in their tacos, and a local gem in North Carolina fires up monster barbecue. S36, Ep5
Flavor World. Guy Fieri dives into cultural cuisine starting in Phoenix, Arizona. Then, he tries authentic Argentine in Boulder, Colorado, and Ethiopian meats in Memphis, Tennessee. S38, Ep15
Bird, Belly And Beef. Guy Fieri seeks out flavourful meat! He checks out Mexican pork sandwiches in Memphis, Tennessee, and rich short ribs in St. Simons Island, Georgia. S38, Ep16
